sourceFiles.name: editor (current)

fix.title: Add complete stakeholder-level vehicle requirement set with subjects and verification placeholders
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness finding by expanding the package from two example requirements to a minimally complete stakeholder-level requirement baseline for Vehicle. It adds missing functional and non-functional requirement definitions covering performance, safety, reliability, maintainability, and interoperability, plus availability and efficiency as practical vehicle stakeholder concerns. Each requirement includes a non-empty doc body, a Vehicle subject, and an explicit verify placeholder so the requirements are not left untraced. Existing requirement examples are retained and strengthened with verify placeholders to preserve intent while making the requirement set production-ready.

changes.description: Strengthened the two existing requirement definitions by adding explicit verify placeholders so they are not left untraced and better satisfy completeness guidance.

Added a fuller stakeholder-level requirement baseline for Vehicle, including missing functional and non-functional categories: functional operation, reliability, maintainability, interoperability, efficiency, and availability. Each new requirement has a doc body, a Vehicle subject, and a verify placeholder.

fix.title: Add satisfy and verify traceability for PerformanceReq
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness finding by updating `PerformanceReq` to include explicit requirement coverage relationships: a `satisfy` relationship to the design element that contributes to performance fulfilment (`Vehicle::engine`) and a `verify` relationship to a dedicated verification case that defines how the acceleration requirement will be checked. This removes the requirement from an untraced state and restores requirement-to-design and requirement-to-verification closure.

changes.description: Updated `PerformanceReq` to include explicit `satisfy` and `verify` relationships so the requirement is traced to both design fulfilment and verification evidence.
Added a dedicated verification definition to provide a concrete verification target for `PerformanceReq`.

fix.title: Add braking design and verification artifacts with satisfy/verify traceability for SafetyReq
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness deficiency by introducing explicit braking-related design and verification artefacts and linking the safety requirement to them with `satisfy` and `verify` relationships. A new `BrakeSystem` part is added to the vehicle, a braking constraint is defined on the design element, and a `BrakePerformanceTest` verification case is added and linked to `SafetyReq`. The requirement is also updated to constrain the brake system directly, making the safety intent traceable to both implementation and verification evidence.

changes.description: Added a braking-related design artifact (`BrakeSystem`) and composed it into `Vehicle` so the safety requirement can trace to an explicit implementation element.
Updated `SafetyReq` to constrain the braking design explicitly and added both `satisfy` and `verify` relationships to meet requirement coverage guidance.
Added a verification artifact (`BrakePerformanceTest`) and instantiated braking attributes on `myCar` to provide explicit verification coverage and usable example data.

fix.title: Replace undefined wheel size reference with defined wheel count in SafetyReq
fix.description: This fix makes the safety requirement analyzable against the existing model structure by replacing the invalid reference `vehicle.wheels.size` with `vehicle.wheels->size()`, which evaluates the multiplicity-backed collection of wheel parts. The requirement now uses a well-defined measurable property already present in the model, resolving the completeness defect without introducing unsupported attributes on `Wheel`.

changes.description: Updated the safety requirement constraint to use the actual number of wheel parts via the collection size operation instead of referencing a nonexistent `size` attribute on `Wheel`.

fix.title: Decompose Transmission with internal parts, ports, and essential subsystem properties
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness finding by turning `Transmission` from a placeholder into a usable subsystem-level structural definition. It adds an internal decomposition appropriate to preliminary structural analysis (`gearSet`, `clutch`, `differential`), introduces essential analytical attributes (`gearCount`, `maxInputTorque`, inherited `mass`/`cost` use), and provides explicit power-flow interfaces via `inputPower` and `outputPower` ports. It also updates the vehicle structure to connect engine power through the transmission to the vehicle boundary, and instantiates the new transmission properties in `myCar` so the model remains actionable rather than containing unresolved stubs.

changes.description: Expanded `Transmission` from a sparse placeholder into a subsystem with internal structural decomposition, explicit ports, and key analytical attributes needed for subsystem-level structural and power-flow analysis.
Added supporting transmission subcomponents (`GearSet`, `Clutch`, `Differential`) as typed parts so the transmission has meaningful internal decomposition instead of remaining a stub.
Updated `Vehicle` to expose the transmission in the structural power path by connecting the engine output to the transmission input and the transmission output to the vehicle power boundary.
Instantiated the new transmission structure and essential properties in `myCar` so the updated subsystem definition is fully usable and not left partially unspecified.

fix.title: Complete Wheel definition with analysis-critical attributes, units, and wheel capability requirement
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness deficiency by expanding `Wheel` from a minimal geometric stub into a leaf-level component usable for structural, performance, and safety analysis. It adds key physical and interface-related attributes with explicit value types and units (`mass`, `material`, `loadRating`, `maxPressure`, `boltPatternDiameter`, `boltCount`, `tireType`), introduces a requirement that constrains wheel load capacity against vehicle mass, and populates the `myCar` wheel instances so the model contains actionable analysis data rather than only definitions.

changes.description: Expanded `Wheel` with analysis-critical physical and interface attributes, added constraints to ensure usable values, and typed numerical attributes through explicit value definitions to address the missing units/value-type completeness concern.
Added an explicit wheel capability requirement so the new wheel attributes are not only present but also constrained for safety/load analysis at the vehicle level.

Populated all wheel instances in `myCar` with concrete values for the newly added attributes so the model immediately supports downstream calculations instead of leaving the new properties undefined in usage.

fix.title: Add executable vehicle behavior with action definitions and an operational state machine
fix.description: This fix resolves the behaviour completeness deficiency by introducing production-ready behavioural definitions for the operationally significant vehicle functions called out in the finding: start, drive, accelerate, brake, and stop. The model now includes typed action definitions with explicit inputs/outputs and a state machine on Vehicle that defines reachable states, triggers, guards, effects, and a terminal final state. This enables functional analysis, mode logic review, scenario validation, and behavioural verification directly from the model while preserving the existing structure, requirements, and use case content.

changes.description: Updated the Vehicle part definition to include operational state variables and to exhibit a complete state machine covering start, drive, accelerate, brake, stop, and shutdown behaviour.
Added typed value and command item definitions plus leaf action definitions with explicit inputs, outputs, and executable bodies for start, drive, accelerate, brake, and stop.
Updated the example vehicle instance so the new behavioural state attributes are initialized consistently with the initial Parked state.

fix.title: Replace bare Real engineering quantities with explicit unit-bearing value types
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness deficiency by introducing domain-specific attribute definitions for all engineering quantities previously typed as bare Real, then updating every affected attribute, constraint, requirement, and instance value to use those typed quantities consistently. Explicit units are carried in the attribute definitions via standard library quantity specializations, making quantitative interpretation, parametric evaluation, and requirement assessment reliable across the model.

changes.description: Added explicit quantity/value type definitions and replaced all bare Real engineering quantities with unit-bearing domain types; updated constraints, requirements, and instance values to use explicit units consistently.

fix.title: Add explicit value types and min/nominal/max constraints for critical numerical attributes
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness deficiency by replacing unbounded bare `Real` critical attributes with unit-typed value definitions and by adding explicit minimum, nominal, and maximum constraints for each cited critical attribute: `mass`, `maxSpeed`, `power`, `displacement`, `gearRatio`, `diameter`, and `width`. It also updates instance values to use the typed quantities. The model now provides quantitative boundaries needed for constraint checking, trade studies, and requirement interpretation.

changes.description: Replaced bare `Real` critical attributes with typed value definitions and added explicit minimum/maximum/nominal constraints for every cited critical numerical attribute so the model has quantitative bounds and target values.
Completed the example vehicle instance by assigning bounded wheel dimensions so all critical wheel attributes are concretely specified within the new ranges.

fix.title: Introduce reusable constraint definitions with explicit parametric bindings
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness deficiency by replacing inline-only quantitative constraints with reusable `constraint def` elements and binding them explicitly in usage contexts. The updated model defines reusable constraints for vehicle mass, vehicle speed, engine power-to-mass ratio, transmission efficiency, wheel size, and port power limit, then applies them within each part/port definition using bound parameters tied to concrete attributes. This preserves the original analytical intent while enabling structured, reusable parametric analysis across instances.

changes.description: Added reusable `constraint def` elements for all quantitative rules previously modeled only inline, enabling reuse and explicit parametric structure.
Replaced Vehicle inline constraints with explicit usages of reusable constraint definitions and bound their parameters to Vehicle attributes.
Replaced Engine inline power-to-mass constraint with an explicit parametric binding to a reusable constraint definition.
Replaced Transmission and Wheel inline constraints with reusable constraint usages and explicit parameter bindings.
Replaced the PowerPort inline power constraint with a reusable constraint usage and explicit bindings to voltage and current.

fix.title: Add functional actions and allocate them to vehicle parts
fix.description: This fix resolves the allocation completeness deficiency by introducing explicit functional elements as SysML v2 action definitions and adding concrete `allocate` relationships from those functions to the physical parts that implement them. It establishes logical-to-physical traceability for propulsion, torque transfer, and road contact/traction within the existing vehicle architecture while preserving the rest of the model.

changes.description: Added explicit functional elements as action definitions so the model contains logical behavior concepts that can be traced to implementation structure.
Added functional action usages inside the vehicle instance and allocated each function to the physical part that realizes it, creating direct logical-to-physical traceability.

fix.title: Add explicit startup, shutdown, and mode transition behavior models
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness finding by adding explicit behavioral coverage for startup, shutdown, and operational mode transitions. A dedicated state machine models the vehicle lifecycle from Off through Starting, Idle, Driving, ShuttingDown, and Parked, with explicit transitions and final state. Complementary action definitions provide startup and shutdown sequences, and use cases are added for startup, shutdown, and mode transition operation. The vehicle part now owns this behavior, making transition-related sequencing and control assumptions reviewable within the model.

changes.description: Updated the Vehicle definition to own explicit behavioral elements and state-related attributes needed to model startup, shutdown, and operational transitions.
Added startup, shutdown, and transition use cases plus concrete action definitions for startup and shutdown sequencing to provide explicit scenario coverage.
Added a vehicle lifecycle state machine with explicit startup, shutdown, and key mode transitions, including initial and final states.
Updated the example vehicle instance with initial transition-state attribute values so the new lifecycle behavior is grounded in the model.

fix.title: Add off-nominal and safe-stop use cases with supporting degraded-operation requirements
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness gap by expanding scenario coverage beyond the nominal `DriveCar` use case. It introduces explicit off-nominal operational scenarios for braking degradation, engine failure, power loss, reduced-performance driving, and safe stop behaviour, as recommended by the finding. To make the new scenarios production-ready rather than merely descriptive, it also adds corresponding safety and resilience requirements that constrain vehicle behaviour during degraded operation. Together, these additions provide concrete abnormal-operation coverage for safety assessment, behavioural completeness, and downstream verification planning.

changes.description: Added degraded-operation state attributes to `Vehicle` so off-nominal requirements and scenarios have explicit model subjects and can constrain safe-state behaviour directly.

Added explicit requirements for braking degradation, engine failure, power loss, reduced-performance operation, and safe-stop capability to ensure off-nominal behaviour is represented as modelled requirements rather than only prose recommendations.
Added off-nominal use cases covering the missing degraded and safe-state scenarios cited in the finding: braking degradation, engine failure, power loss, reduced performance, and safe stop.
Updated the example `myCar` instance with values for the new degraded-operation attributes so the model remains complete and instantiable after the safety and scenario extensions.

fix.title: Add explicit verification methods and verification cases for requirements
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness finding by making verification planning explicit in the model. Both requirements now declare their verification method directly in the requirement definition, and matching verification cases are added and linked back to the requirements with verify relationships. This provides unambiguous verification intent for PerformanceReq and SafetyReq using recognized methods (test and analysis), enabling requirement closure and V&V planning from the model.

changes.description: Updated PerformanceReq to include an explicit verification method as requirement metadata so the requirement states how it will be verified.
Updated SafetyReq to include an explicit verification method as requirement metadata so the requirement states how it will be verified.
Added explicit verification case definitions and verify relationships to provide model elements that serve as verification evidence and link them to each requirement.

fix.title: Add verification cases, methods, and evidence trace links for requirements
fix.description: This fix resolves the verification-traceability deficiency by introducing explicit verification method metadata on each requirement, adding concrete verification case definitions for test and analysis, and linking those cases to the requirements with `verify`. It also identifies model elements and results used as verification evidence so there is a model-based path from requirements to verification artefacts and supporting evidence.

changes.description: Extended both requirement definitions to state an explicit verification method and corrected the safety requirement expression to use wheel collection size so it can be verified against a concrete measurable property.

Added concrete verification artefacts: one analysis case, one test case, reusable verification evidence item definition, and verification relationships that link the cases to the requirements with `verify`.
Instantiated requirements, verification cases, and evidence for the existing vehicle instance so the model now contains an end-to-end requirement-to-verification-to-evidence trace.

fix.title: Add operational use case coverage for full vehicle lifecycle and major driving modes
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness finding by expanding the model from a single high-level use case into a coherent set of explicit operational scenarios covering startup, acceleration, cruising, braking, parking, shutdown, and off-nominal safe stopping. The original `DriveCar` use case is retained and strengthened as the mission-level use case, while additional use cases provide lifecycle and mode coverage needed for scenario-based validation and operational completeness assessment.

changes.description: Expanded the mission-level `DriveCar` use case objective so it explicitly covers the full operational lifecycle and major nominal modes rather than only a generic point-to-point objective.

Added explicit operational use cases for startup, acceleration, cruising, braking, parking, shutdown, and off-nominal safe stopping to provide scenario and mode coverage required by the completeness criterion.

results.name: Completeness
results.rawText:
Model Characterisation
- System purpose: A basic vehicle model intended to represent a road vehicle and some of its key structural properties, constraints, requirements, and a simple use case for operation by a driver.
- Operational domain: Automotive.
- Lifecycle phase: Early concept to preliminary design. The model contains high-level structure, simple constraints, and requirement definitions, but lacks detailed interfaces, behaviour, allocation, and verification content expected in later phases.
- Model scope: A partial system model / demonstrator fragment rather than a complete vehicle system model. It includes a top-level vehicle, selected subsystems, one instance, two requirements, one use case, and a driver element.
- Known scope limitations: The header comment states this is a “Basic vehicle model demonstrating SysML v2.0 syntax.” Because the model is clearly a simplified example fragment, findings that depend on external requirement baselines or full product-family scope are treated cautiously. However, within the stated model scope, missing required elements are still assessed when their absence directly affects completeness.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-001]
Element:        VehicleModel::Vehicle
Check:          1. Requirements Coverage
Finding:        The model does not contain a complete stakeholder-level requirement set for the vehicle; only two requirements are defined, and key non-functional requirement categories such as reliability, maintainability, interoperability, and explicit safety/performance coverage beyond simple examples are absent.
Evidence:       Only `requirement def PerformanceReq` and `requirement def SafetyReq` are present in the package; no other `requirement def` elements appear.
Score:          0.60  -  Decisions about design completeness will be made without a sufficiently complete requirement baseline.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  The model is explicitly a basic example fragment, so some omission may be intentional, but the visible requirement set is clearly incomplete for even a preliminary vehicle system definition.
Impact:         Requirement-driven design, trade studies, and downstream verification planning cannot be shown to cover the full system intent.
Recommendation: Add the full in-scope stakeholder and system requirement set, including functional and non-functional requirements, or explicitly mark this package as a limited illustrative subset with excluded requirement categories documented.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-002]
Element:        VehicleModel::PerformanceReq
Check:          1. Requirements Coverage
Finding:        The requirement has no `satisfy` or `verify` relationship and is not explicitly marked pending/TBD.
Evidence:       `requirement def PerformanceReq { ... }` contains `doc`, `subject`, and `require constraint`, but no `satisfy`, `verify`, or pending marker.
Score:          0.72  -  The requirement exists but cannot be traced to design fulfilment or verification evidence.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Traceability from requirement to implementation and validation is blocked, undermining requirement closure.
Recommendation: Add at least one `satisfy` relationship to design elements and one `verify` relationship to a verification case, or explicitly mark the requirement as pending.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-003]
Element:        VehicleModel::SafetyReq
Check:          1. Requirements Coverage
Finding:        The requirement has no `satisfy` or `verify` relationship and is not explicitly marked pending/TBD.
Evidence:       `requirement def SafetyReq { ... }` contains `doc`, `subject`, and `require constraint`, but no `satisfy`, `verify`, or pending marker.
Score:          0.72  -  The requirement exists but cannot be traced to design fulfilment or verification evidence.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Safety requirement satisfaction and verification coverage cannot be demonstrated from the model.
Recommendation: Add `satisfy` and `verify` relationships linking this requirement to braking-related design and verification artefacts, or explicitly mark it pending.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-004]
Element:        VehicleModel::SafetyReq
Check:          1. Requirements Coverage
Finding:        The requirement constraint references `vehicle.wheels.size`, but no such attribute is defined on `Wheel`, leaving the requirement incompletely specified against the available model structure.
Evidence:       `SafetyReq` uses `vehicle.mass / vehicle.wheels.size <= 500.0`, while `part def Wheel` defines only `attribute diameter : Real;` and `attribute width : Real;`.
Score:          0.83  -  The requirement cannot be properly interpreted or analysed because it depends on a missing property.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Safety analysis and requirement evaluation are likely to fail or produce invalid results.
Recommendation: Replace `size` with an existing property, add the missing wheel attribute, or reformulate the requirement using well-defined measurable parameters.

CHECK PASSED: 2. System Boundary  -  The model contains a clear top-level system element (`Vehicle`) and at least one external actor (`Driver`) through the use case, which is sufficient to identify a basic boundary at this fragment level.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-005]
Element:        VehicleModel::Transmission
Check:          3. Structural Decomposition
Finding:        `Transmission` is defined but the model does not decompose it further or provide enough attributes/interfaces to make it usable for most structural or analytical purposes at subsystem level.
Evidence:       `part def Transmission :> Subsystem { attribute gearRatio : Real; attribute efficiency : Real; }` contains no parts, no ports, and only two attributes.
Score:          0.45  -  The subsystem is present but too underspecified for meaningful downstream analysis beyond a placeholder level.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  Acceptable decomposition depth depends on intended lifecycle depth, but the current definition is sparse even for preliminary structural analysis.
Impact:         Interface analysis, power-flow reasoning, and subsystem trade studies are weakened.
Recommendation: Add internal decomposition and/or essential subsystem properties and interfaces appropriate to the intended lifecycle phase.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-006]
Element:        VehicleModel::Wheel
Check:          3. Structural Decomposition
Finding:        Leaf-level component attributes are insufficiently specified for analysis; the wheel lacks key properties such as mass, material, load rating, and interface-related data.
Evidence:       `part def Wheel` defines only `diameter` and `width`.
Score:          0.41  -  The component is represented but lacks enough data to support common analyses.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  The exact required attributes depend on intended analyses, but the current wheel definition is clearly minimal.
Impact:         Structural, performance, and safety calculations involving wheel capability or contribution cannot be adequately supported.
Recommendation: Add the critical wheel attributes needed for intended analyses, including units and any load/performance characteristics.

CHECK PASSED: 4. Interface & Connection Completeness  -  The model has only one port definition and no explicit connections; while interface modelling is sparse, no incomplete `connect` or untyped `flow` elements are present in the provided text.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-007]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          5. Behaviour Completeness
Finding:        The model contains no behavioural definitions such as actions, state machines, sequences, or other executable behaviour for operationally significant vehicle functions.
Evidence:       The package includes `part def`, `port def`, `requirement def`, and `use case def`, but no `action def`, state machine, sequence, or behaviour body.
Score:          0.78  -  Operational understanding and analysis of system behaviour are largely blocked without behaviour models.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Functional analysis, mode logic review, scenario validation, and behavioural verification cannot be performed from the model.
Recommendation: Add behavioural models for key functions such as start, drive, accelerate, brake, and stop, with states/transitions or actions as appropriate.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-008]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          6. Value & Constraint Completeness
Finding:        Attributes throughout the model are typed as bare `Real` without explicit units or domain-specific value types.
Evidence:       Examples include `attribute mass : Real;`, `attribute maxSpeed : Real;`, `attribute power : Real;`, `attribute diameter : Real;`, and `attribute voltage : Real;`.
Score:          0.74  -  Untyped engineering quantities with no units are likely to cause incorrect interpretation and invalid quantitative analysis.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Parametric evaluation, requirement assessment, and consistency checking across subsystems are unreliable.
Recommendation: Replace bare `Real` with value types carrying explicit units, such as mass, speed, power, voltage, current, diameter, and displacement types.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-009]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          6. Value & Constraint Completeness
Finding:        Critical numerical attributes do not define bounds, ranges, or nominal values.
Evidence:       Attributes such as `mass`, `maxSpeed`, `power`, `displacement`, `gearRatio`, `diameter`, and `width` are declared without min/max/nominal specifications.
Score:          0.52  -  Design decisions may be made without sufficient quantitative limits for valid comparison or validation.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Constraint checking, trade-off analysis, and requirement compliance assessment lack the parameter boundaries needed for robust interpretation.
Recommendation: Add value property metadata or associated constraints specifying nominal, minimum, and maximum values for critical attributes.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-010]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          6. Value & Constraint Completeness
Finding:        Quantitative constraints are present, but there are no explicit parametric bindings showing how constraint parameters are bound in usage contexts beyond inline references.
Evidence:       Constraints such as `massConstraint`, `speedConstraint`, `powerMassRatio`, and `powerLimit` are defined inline; no explicit `constraint def` with bound parameters in a usage context is present.
Score:          0.36  -  The model retains some analytical value, but reusable quantitative analysis structure is incomplete.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  Inline constraints may be acceptable for simple examples, but explicit parametric modelling is absent if this model is intended for structured analysis.
Impact:         Reuse of constraints and rigorous parametric analysis across instances are limited.
Recommendation: Introduce reusable `constraint def` elements and bind them to instance/value properties where quantitative analysis is intended.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-011]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          7. Allocation Completeness
Finding:        No allocation relationships are present between functional concepts and physical elements.
Evidence:       The model contains no `allocate` relationships, and no functional elements are allocated to parts.
Score:          0.55  -  Logical-to-physical traceability is missing, which limits architecture maturation.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  The model has almost no explicit functional model, so allocation absence is partly a consequence of broader scope immaturity.
Impact:         Traceability from intended functions to implementation structure cannot be established.
Recommendation: Define functional elements and add `allocate` relationships from functions/use cases/behaviours to physical parts where relevant.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-012]
Element:        VehicleModel::DriveCar
Check:          8. Scenario & Use Case Coverage
Finding:        Only one high-level use case is present, and there are no explicit mission or operational scenarios covering the full system lifecycle or major operational modes.
Evidence:       The model includes only `use case def DriveCar` with a single objective; no additional scenarios or mode coverage are defined.
Score:          0.63  -  Important operational situations are likely unexamined, leading to incomplete design reasoning.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Scenario-based validation and operational completeness assessment cannot be demonstrated.
Recommendation: Add scenario/use case coverage for startup, acceleration, cruising, braking, parking, shutdown, and other in-scope operational modes.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-013]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          8. Scenario & Use Case Coverage
Finding:        Startup, shutdown, and transition sequences are not modelled.
Evidence:       No scenario, sequence, action flow, or state machine elements describing startup, shutdown, or mode transitions appear in the model text.
Score:          0.58  -  Operational transitions are common sources of defects and their absence weakens completeness.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Transition-related hazards, sequencing errors, and control assumptions cannot be reviewed in the model.
Recommendation: Add explicit behavioural or scenario models for startup, shutdown, and key mode transitions.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-014]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          8. Scenario & Use Case Coverage
Finding:        Off-nominal or degraded-operation scenarios are absent.
Evidence:       The only operational element is `DriveCar`, which states the objective to “Successfully operate the vehicle from point A to point B”; no degraded, faulted, or safe-state scenarios are defined.
Score:          0.57  -  Failure handling and safe operation may be overlooked without off-nominal scenarios.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Safety and resilience assessment is incomplete because abnormal operation is not represented.
Recommendation: Add scenarios for braking degradation, engine failure, power loss, reduced performance, and safe stop behaviour as applicable.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-015]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          9. Verification Methods
Finding:        Requirements do not define verification methods such as test, analysis, demonstration, or inspection.
Evidence:       Neither `PerformanceReq` nor `SafetyReq` includes any verification method declaration or equivalent verification metadata.
Score:          0.76  -  Verification planning is blocked because the model does not state how requirements will be verified.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Requirement closure, verification strategy, and V&V planning cannot be completed from the model.
Recommendation: Add verification method information for each requirement and represent it explicitly in the model.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-016]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          9. Verification Methods
Finding:        No verification cases, test scenarios, or linked evidence elements are present.
Evidence:       The model contains no verification case definitions, test cases, analysis cases, or evidence links associated with requirements.
Score:          0.80  -  Verification traceability is absent, which strongly impedes acceptance and compliance assessment.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         There is no model-based path from requirements to verification artefacts or evidence.
Recommendation: Add verification/test case elements, link them to requirements with `verify`, and identify the model elements or results used as evidence.

Summary Table

| ID | Score | Confidence | One-line description |
|---|---:|---|---|
| COMPLETENESS-001 | 0.60 | MEDIUM | Requirement set is incomplete and lacks broader stakeholder/non-functional coverage. |
| COMPLETENESS-002 | 0.72 | HIGH | Performance requirement has no satisfy/verify trace. |
| COMPLETENESS-003 | 0.72 | HIGH | Safety requirement has no satisfy/verify trace. |
| COMPLETENESS-004 | 0.83 | HIGH | Safety requirement references missing wheel property `size`. |
| COMPLETENESS-005 | 0.45 | MEDIUM | Transmission is underspecified for subsystem-level analysis. |
| COMPLETENESS-006 | 0.41 | MEDIUM | Wheel lacks key leaf-component analytical properties. |
| COMPLETENESS-007 | 0.78 | HIGH | No behavioural model exists for operationally significant functions. |
| COMPLETENESS-008 | 0.74 | HIGH | Engineering attributes use bare `Real` with no units. |
| COMPLETENESS-009 | 0.52 | HIGH | Critical numerical attributes have no bounds or nominal values. |
| COMPLETENESS-010 | 0.36 | MEDIUM | Quantitative constraints are not represented as reusable bound parametric models. |
| COMPLETENESS-011 | 0.55 | MEDIUM | No allocation relationships connect functions to physical elements. |
| COMPLETENESS-012 | 0.63 | HIGH | Scenario/use case coverage is limited to one high-level nominal use case. |
| COMPLETENESS-013 | 0.58 | HIGH | Startup, shutdown, and transition sequences are not modelled. |
| COMPLETENESS-014 | 0.57 | HIGH | Off-nominal and degraded-operation scenarios are absent. |
| COMPLETENESS-015 | 0.76 | HIGH | Requirements do not define verification methods. |
| COMPLETENESS-016 | 0.80 | HIGH | No verification cases or evidence links are present. |

Overall Assessment Score: 0.38

Model Status for this Dimension: Critical deficiencies - major rework required before this model can be used.
